title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Importance & Methods of ULIP Renewal
What is ULIP?
A ULIP is a market-linked life insurance policy wherein the invested premiums are directed to specific funds (that the policyholder chooses). Each fund contains a diversified portfolio of securities that are traded on the financial market and are  associated with market risks. As the market performs, the value of the portfolio changes and so does your investment.
ULIPs also offer flexible features of partial withdrawal, fund switching, premium redirection, top-ups, etc. to allow you to manage your investments effectively. There’s a lock-in period of 5 years after which you are allowed to surrender the policy and avail of the fund value, subject to the terms and conditions of the policy.
Being life insurance plans, ULIPs have insurance coverage as well. The life insured is covered during the policy tenure. If the insured passes away before maturity, a death benefit is paid. Alternatively, if the insured survives the chosen policy tenure, the maturity benefit is paid.
There are different types of ULIPs available in the market and you can choose one or more of these plans depending on your financial goals. The sum assured is usually expressed as a multiple of the annual or single premium paid for the policy.
Overview of ULIP renewal
ULIPs are long-term insurance plans wherein the tenure usually starts at 5 years and goes up to the specified tenure. Some ULIPs even run till 100 or 99 years of age allowing lifelong protection.
The premiums for ULIPs can be paid in three ways:
Single premium – The premium is payable only once when you buy the ULIP
Limited premium – The premium is paid for a limited tenure which is lower than the total policy tenure. For instance, in a 15-year ULIP policy, a limited premium tenure might be of 10 years only. You pay for 10 years but enjoy the coverage for 15 years.
Regular premium – wherein you pay the premium throughout the chosen policy tenure. So, if you choose a policy tenure of 15 years, you have to pay the premium for 15 years.
Under limited and regular premium policies, you have to pay the premiums regularly, for the stipulated tenure. After the payment of the first premium, the payment of the subsequent premium is called ULIP renewal. As you pay the premium on the due date, the coverage is renewed and it runs for the duration for which the premium has been duly paid.
Importance/ significance of renewing your ULIP policy
Renewing your ULIP policy is important for some of the following reasons –
Continued coverage
As you renew the ULIP regularly, you can enjoy complete insurance protection allowed by the policy. In the case of an unforeseen event, the ULIP will pay the death benefit to provide financial security to your family.
Market-linked returns
As you keep paying the premiums, your fund value keeps on increasing. This increases your investments and you can earn market-linked returns on the enhanced investments.
Avoid lapse and reduced benefits
If the ULIP is not renewed on time, it may lapse. A lapsed policy has various implications. Have a look –
Lapse in the first 5 years – if you fail to renew the plan within the lock-in period of 5 years, your fund value would be transferred to the discontinued policy fund. The money would stay invested in the fund till the lock-in period is completed. The charges would be deducted from the fund value which would further reduce the value of your investments. After the lock-in period is over, the policy would be deemed to be surrendered. You would receive the available fund value and the coverage would be terminated1.
Lapse after the first 5 years – if the lock-in period is over and you discontinue premium payments, your policy would be converted to a reduced paid-up policy. The policy shall continue to be in reduced paid-up status without rider cover, if any.
The fund would remain invested and you would continue generating market linked returns on the same. The applicable charges, if any would be deducted as per the terms & conditions of the policy. You can choose to continue the paid-up policy till maturity and receive the available fund value or surrender the policy before maturity and get the surrender value1.
Reduction in death benefit – ULIPs usually pay higher of the sum assured or the available fund value if the insured dies. While the above-mentioned points outline what happens to the fund value, the sum assured is also reduced. Reduction in the sum assured is done in proportion to the number of premiums paid and the number of premiums payable. This is called the paid-up sum assured and it is used when assessing the death benefit1.
For instance, say you bought a 20-year ULIP and renewed it for 10 years. The sum assured was ₹10 lakhs. If you do not renew the coverage, the paid-up sum assured would be calculated as – 10 years/20 years X ₹10 lakhs = ₹5 lakhs
By renewing on time, you can avoid these circumstances and enjoy full benefits.
Tax benefits on premium
ULIP premiums are allowed as a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, up to ₹1.5 lakhs2, under old tax regime. By renewing the policy on time, you can continue enjoying the tax benefits in each financial year when the ULIP premiums are payable.
Different Ways to Renew your ULIP
Renewal of a ULIP can be done using two primary modes – the online mode and the offline mode. Each mode offers various alternatives for premium payment to renew the coverage. Have a look –
Online Mode of Renewal
Premium payment can be done through the following means -
Credit card
Debit card
Mobile wallets
UPI
NEFT
RTGS
IMPS
Direct debit from your bank account
Online fund transfers, etc.
Offline Mode of Renewal
Premium can be paid through the following means –
Cash
Cheque
Demand draft
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP online
When renewing your ULIP using the online premium payment mode, here are a few things to keep in mind:
Ensure your bank allows the net banking facility for online fund transfers through your account. Also, register for the facility for seamless payments.
If you are using UPI, link your bank account to your UPI application and create UPI ID and maintain optimal funds in the account for a successful transaction
In the case of mobile wallets, ensure that the wallet has sufficient balance to pay the premium
Credit and debit cards should be used carefully with the details entered correctly
You can also make the payment on the insurer’s website, it is secured and encrypted
Avoid using public Wi-Fi for payment as it is prone to cyberattacks
Keep your password/PIN/OTP confidential. Do not share it with anyone, even a company representative
Use a fast internet connection to allow the transaction to go through without any lags or failures
Beware of cyberattacks and crimes and take preventive measures to avoid them like installing antimalware in your system, etc.
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP offline
If you are renewing the ULIP offline, here are some things to remember –
If you are paying in cash, remember that the maximum premium that would be accepted in cash would be ₹50,0003. If the premium is higher, choose to pay via a cheque or demand draft
If paying through a cheque, maintain sufficient funds in your bank account to avoid cheque bounce
Mention the correct policy number on the cheque or demand draft
Draw the cheque or draft in favour of the insurance company mentioning the correct name
Conclusion
A ULIP is a beneficial tool in your financial portfolio that offers insurance protection and investment opportunity. The plan can help in creating wealth for your goals so that you can achieve them easily. ULIPs run for longer tenures and regular renewals become important to enjoy the coverage that the policy offers. So, renew your ULIPs on time by paying the premiums online or offline. If you miss the due date, use the grace period and pay the premium to enjoy complete coverage.
Section on FAQs
How can I renew my ULIP policy?
You can renew your ULIP policy by paying the due premium online or offline, within the due date or within the allowed grace period or revival period.
Should I renew my ULIP policy?
It is recommended to renew your ULIP policy to enjoy complete insurance coverage, corpus creation through market-linked returns and tax benefits. Moreover, with regular renewals, you can build a suitable corpus for your financial goals.
Why is ULIP renewal important?
ULIP renewal is important because of the various benefits that it provides. These benefits include tax savings, full insurance protection, market-linked returns through enhanced investments and more.

word_comments.author: Ayushi Sharma/Pune HO/Legal Compliance and FPU/Life
word_comments.comment: This is an excerpt from Unit linked Product Regulations, pls align the section with this. You may write in brief but ensure all the relevant details are being covered-Discontinuance of Policy after the lock-in-Period: a) For other than Single Premium Policies: i) Upon expiry of the grace period, in case of discontinuance of policy due to non-payment of premium after lock-in period, the policy shall be converted into a reduced paid up policy with the paid-up sum assured i.e. original sum assured multiplied by the total number of premiums paid to the original number of premiums payable as per the terms and conditions of the policy. The policy shall continue to be in reduced paid-up status without rider cover, if any. All charges as per terms and conditions of the policy may be deducted during the revival period. However, the mortality charges shall be deducted based on the reduced paid up sum assured only. ii) On such discontinuance, Insurer shall communicate the status of the policy, within three months of the first unpaid premium, to the policyholder and provide the following options: (1) To revive the policy within the revival period of three years, or (2) Complete withdrawal of the policy. iii) In case the policyholder opts for (1) of Regulation 14(a)(ii) under these Regulations above but does not revive the policy during the revival period, the fund value shall be paid to the policyholder at the end of the revival period. iv) In case the policyholder does not exercise any option as set out above, the policy shall continue to be in reduced paid up status. At the end of the revival period the proceeds of the policy fund shall be paid to the policyholder and the policy shall terminate. 35 v) However, the policyholder has an option to surrender the policy anytime and proceeds of the policy fund shall be payable. b) In case of Single Premium Policies, the policyholder has an option to surrender the policy any time. Upon receipt of request for surrender, the fund value as on date of surrender shall be payable. 15) Revival of a discontinued Policy after lock-in Period a) The policyholder can revive the policy, in accordance with sub-regulation (u) of Regulation 3. Where the policyholder revives the policy, the policy shall be revived restoring the original risk cover in accordance with the terms and conditions of the policy. b) The insurer, at the time of revival: i) shall collect all due and unpaid premiums under base plan without charging any interest or fee. The rider may also be revived at the option of the policyholders. ii) may levy premium allocation charge as applicable. The guarantee charges may be deducted, if guarantee continues to be applicable. iii) No other charges shall be levied
